exampleSentencesTranslated: I often help my mother cook dinner in the evening.
My mother really likes planting flowers in front of the house.
She bought a small gift for her mother on her birthday.
notes_md:
**mẹ (me)** = mother. It means your mom, used for your own mother or someone else’s.

It’s a family noun, so you usually don’t use classifiers with it. To say whose mom, just put a pronoun after it.  

You can also call your mom directly with it (often with ơi to get her attention).  

In the South, many people say **má** instead of **mẹ** — same meaning, just regional.

It’s a family noun, so you usually don’t use classifiers with it. To say whose mom, just put a pronoun after it.

exampleSentences: Em thường giúp me nấu cơm vào buổi tối.
Me của anh rất thích trồng hoa trước nhà.
Chị đã mua một món quà nhỏ tặng me nhân dịp sinh nhật.
